description: Leaving the default chunk interval on a high-ingest table, producing either giant working sets or millions of tiny chunks.
body: Failure mode: the default interval is left unchanged on a table whose ingest rate is far from the default assumption, so chunks are either too large (working set won't fit memory) or too many (planning time explodes). Detection signals: high memory use from one active chunk, or slow query planning from excessive chunk count. Do not assume the default fits every ingest rate. Safer fallback: measure chunk size and count under real load and set the interval so recent data fits 1-2 chunks and typical queries touch few.
